hook: The job post gave the answer key.
subhook: The resume just did not answer it clearly.
weakBullets: Worked on frontend features for internal and customer-facing tools.
Collaborated with designers and backend engineers.
Helped improve application performance and user experience.
beforeBullet: Worked on frontend features for internal and customer-facing tools.
afterBullet: Shipped React and TypeScript checkout components in Next.js, reducing form drop-off 14% and closing 11 accessibility issues.

resumeDocument.name: Avery Patel
resumeDocument.headline: Frontend Engineer
resumeDocument.summary: Frontend engineer with 5 years building product UI, design-system components, checkout flows, and accessibility improvements for SaaS products.

bullets: Worked on frontend features for internal and customer-facing tools.
Collaborated with designers and backend engineers.
Helped improve application performance and user experience.
Maintained shared React components used by billing, onboarding, and account teams.
Shipped React and TypeScript checkout components in Next.js, reduced form drop-off 14%, and closed 11 WCAG issues.
Converted Figma designs into responsive TypeScript UI components.
Triaged customer-reported accessibility issues with QA and support.
Added Playwright regression coverage for checkout and profile flows.

resumeDocument.education: B.S. Computer Science, University of Washington

jobDescription.title: Frontend Software Engineer
jobDescription.summary: TargetCo is hiring a Frontend Software Engineer who can connect practical execution with measurable business outcomes.
responsibilities: Use React and TypeScript to improve team execution and reporting.
Translate work into measurable outcomes tied to Next.js.
Partner cross-functionally and communicate progress clearly to stakeholders.

humanReadBeats.text: Read the exact resume line: Worked on frontend features for internal and customer-facing tools.
The stack is probably real, but the bullet makes me hunt for it.

Point to the visible source evidence in MapleStack experience, later checkout bullet: Shipped React and TypeScript checkout components in Next.js, reduced form drop-off 14%, and closed 11 WCAG issues.
Compare it to the job requirement: React, TypeScript, Next.js.
Explain the visible rubric before the score: 41/100.
Rewrite only the same experience: shipped React and TypeScript checkout work that reduced drop-off 14 percent.

problemPunchline: The role language is visible. The resume is not using it.
teardownPunchline: Low score reason: broad work, missing tool, no result.
fixPunchline: Use the job language only when the experience supports it.

delivery: Sounds like a recruiter reading the resume live.
Short pause after the weak bullet and before the score reason.
No corporate polish, no trailer voice, no product-demo cadence.

voiceover_text: Job post answer key: React, TypeScript, Next.js. Resume line: Worked on frontend features for internal and customer-facing tools. I would circle that because it answers too vaguely. Proof lower down: Shipped React and TypeScript checkout components in Next.js, reduced form drop-off 14%, and closed 11 WCAG issues. I would score it around 41. I would write: shipped React and TypeScript checkout work that reduced drop-off 14 percent. Now the evidence matches, so 90 makes sense. Run the free Signal score before you apply.
